name: node-acorn
title: node-acorn
description:
ECMAScript parser for Node.js
Acorn is a tiny, fast JavaScript parser written in JavaScript.
.
acorn.js defines the main parser interface. The library also comes with a
error-tolerant parser and an abstract syntax tree walker, defined in other
files.
.
This package contains the acorn module for Node.js.
.
This package contains also small pluggins packages acorn-bigint,
acorn-dynamic-import, acorn-jsx, acorn-node, acorn-private-class-elements,
acorn-class-fields, acorn-static-class-features, acorn-export-ns-from,
acorn-numeric-separator, acorn-private-methods.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use acorn because ECMAScript parser for Node.js.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use acorn as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `acorn [--ecma3 | --ecma5 | --ecma6 | --ecma7 | ... | --ecma2015 | --ecma2016] [--tokenize]` as documented upstream for acorn.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair acorn with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `acorn --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install acorn. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `acorn [--ecma3 | --ecma5 | --ecma6 | --ecma7 | ... | --ecma2015 | --ecma2016] [--tokenize]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ECMAScript parser for Node.js.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: ECMAScript parser for Node.js.
